props.alt: The Compelling Investment Case for Climate Adaptation — cover

props.text: Floods and droughts are striking communities harder and more often, and in 2024 alone the world endured 58 disasters that each caused more than a billion dollars in damages. Yet the money available to cope with these impacts falls persistently short, leaving an adaptation funding gap as high as $359 billion per year. In this paper, we set out to reframe adaptation not merely as a way to avoid losses but as one of the smartest investments available today. Drawing on an analysis of 320 adaptation and resilience projects across agriculture, water, health and infrastructure, we apply the 'triple dividend of resilience' framework to capture avoided losses, induced economic development, and additional social and environmental benefits. We find that $1 invested in adaptation can yield more than $10.50 in benefits over 10 years, and that returns are often evenly distributed across all three dividends. Because most appraisals fail to monetize these wider benefits, we conclude that even these strong returns are likely substantial underestimates.
props.heading: Executive summary

items.text: Adaptation Yields High Returns Even when Disasters Don’t Strike
